economic_notes: COVID-19 noted as significant event with uncertain future impact. First Nation has gas bar operations, forestry trust, sawmill trust, and development corporation. OFNLP2008 forestry funding program active.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on three grounds: (1) budgets not approved per PSAS requirements; (2) auditors unable to observe inventory count of $41,705; (3) unable to verify gas bar sales of $802,196 and related accounts receivable of $68,927 due to inaccessible records. Five entities fully consolidated: Amik Business Trust, Amik Forest Renewal Trust, Asiniika Wiikwedong Sawmill Trust, Gi-Niijaasinaanig Obimadiziwinowaa Community Corporation, and BZA Development Inc. Prior year surplus recoveries of $232,162 recorded (amount due back to ISC). Lines of credit total $186,000 outstanding. OSR calculation includes gas bar, fish sales, interest, rentals, admin recovery and other revenue which may include some non-government transfers from organizations like Kinoomaadziwin Education Body, Union of Ontario Indians, Dilico, and AETS — these are treated as non-government transfers for OSR purposes. Schedule 3 was truncated in source document.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
